input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows the force-displacement curve of the impact attenuator obtained from experiments. Assuming the impact attenuator is a standard honeycomb type, and based on this image and rule F.8.7.2 in the rule document, can we use the impact attenuator in the vehicle? First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with ‘Explanation:’). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with ‘Answer:’) that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.8.7.2
            TITLE: the peak load for the type of impact attenuator:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.8.7.2: The peak load for the type of Impact Attenuator:
F.8.7.2 The peak load for the type of Impact Attenuator:  
• Standard Foam Impact Attenuator   95 kN 
• Standard Honeycomb Impact Attenuator 60 kN 
• Tested Impact Attenuator    peak as measured

final_answer:
Explanation: According to Rule F.8.7.2, for an impact attenuator classified as a Standard Honeycomb type, the minimum required peak load is 60 kN. To determine if the attached attenuator can be used, its measured peak force (derived from the provided force-displacement curve) must meet or exceed this specified threshold of 60 kN. If the maximum force recorded on the experimental curve is less than 60 kN, the component does not comply with the technical regulations and cannot be utilized in the vehicle.

Answer: No
